That is the equivalence to which I can think of.

It sucks that they cancelled this, and Good Omens, for what the creator of the original stories did. 

These are tv shows, with own staff and crew, it's only based on his work, he doesn't appear in them, or work on them. There was no reason to cancel the shows, cancelling his books, that would've made a whole lot more sense. 

Now his personal actions have created an outrage from single-minded people who demanded these cancellations, nearly forcing the studios creating them, and costing hundreds of people their jobs. 

Why don't you people think for a fkn second in your lives about who your words and demands rèàlly affect? How does any of this even hurt Neil Gaiman who has fck to do with these shows? ... 😮‍💨😮‍💨

comment_text:
IDK man. I love the graphic novel. But the show keeps making dumb choices. Lucifer was a complete miscast in my opinion, and season one was wokeified. gender and race swapping, like everything in Hollywood. It started strong but fell off fast for me.

Aesthetically, it's good, not great. But little things they do bother me and miss nuances of the comic. For instance, in this trailer the very first shot we get of Destiny is literally his entire face.....the comic almost never shows you his whole face. 

"Why does that matter, who cares!" , well think about what it communicates. He's the one who knows what's going to happen, but you never quite know him. Subtleties like that are really missing in this show in my opinion. Small things that show they really read the comic and understood it.

I hope they get Delirium right, she was an awesome character. Her all black clothing isn't promising though. 


I will say, I think all in all, the guy doing Dream is doing a decent job.

comment_text:
Ya know, I've read so many complaints in this comment section about Gaiman "ruining" everything. I believe it unwise to confuse the work with the man. Many of our greatest artists did terrible things. Picasso was an insane stalker, while Hemingway was a drunk who mercilessly beat his kids. That shouldn't stop us from enjoying the work, separate and apart from the individual that created them. Morpheus, Constantine, the American Gods, we still HAVE those things. You have a right to cherish the art that you love, while scorning the very flawed human being behind them. Art is art, while to be human is to be imperfect. 
And don't forget the creative performers who've brought our most cherished stories to life. They didn't do anything to hurt anyone. All they did was entertain and enlighten us.
